title: CAGRISEMA
description: CagriSema is a fixed-dose combination injectable peptide consisting of semaglutide, a GLP-1 receptor agonist, and cagrilintide, a long-acting amylin analogue. It works by targeting dual satiety pathways in the brain to reduce appetite and slow gastric emptying, primarily used for the treatment of obesity and type 2 diabetes.

research.title: Creatine supplementation is safe, beneficial throughout the lifespan, and should not be restricted
research.summary: Recent comprehensive reviews confirm that creatine is safe for long-term use across all ages and provides significant physical and cognitive benefits.

research.title: Creatine Supplementation: An Update
research.summary: Creatine consistently increases lean muscle mass and exercise capacity while offering potential neuroprotective and recovery benefits.

research.title: Effects of creatine supplementation on muscle strength gains—a meta-analysis and systematic review
research.summary: A meta-analysis confirms that creatine significantly elevates intramuscular stores to enhance energy availability during high-intensity training.

research.title: Single dose creatine improves cognitive performance and induces changes in cerebral high energy phosphates during sleep deprivation
research.summary: High-dose creatine was found to partially reverse cognitive fatigue and metabolic changes in the brain during periods of acute sleep deprivation.

sentiment.summary: Users report overwhelming success with long-term supplementation, citing it as a staple for both physical strength and unexpected 'brain fog' relief.

effects.label: Strength Gains:
effects.description: Consistently rated as the most effective legal supplement for increasing 1RM (one-rep max) and explosive power.

effects.label: Mental Stamina:
effects.description: High-dose protocols (15g+) appear efficacious for users dealing with neurological fatigue or 'long-COVID' symptoms.

effects.label: Consistency:
effects.description: Benefits are most prominent after 2-4 weeks of daily use once muscle stores are fully saturated.

effects.label: Reliability:
effects.description: Unlike many 'hyped' supplements, users frequently note this is one of the few that provides measurable objective results.

dosage.label: Standard Maintenance:
dosage.description: 5g daily is the most common and sustainable dose for general health and athletic performance.

dosage.label: High-Dose Protocol:
dosage.description: 10-15g daily has been utilized by some users to address severe cognitive fatigue or larger muscle masses.

dosage.label: Loading Phase:
dosage.description: 20g daily for 5-7 days is often used to saturate muscles quickly, though many users skip this to avoid GI distress.

dosage.label: Saturation Dosing:
dosage.description: 0.35g/kg of body weight is sometimes used as a single acute dose for temporary cognitive rescue during sleep deprivation.

sideEffects.label: Weight Gain:
sideEffects.description: Initial 2-5 lbs increase due to intracellular water retention, which some users mistake for fat gain.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Distress:
sideEffects.description: Occasional bloating or diarrhea if taken in large doses on an empty stomach without enough water.

sideEffects.label: Mineral Depletion:
sideEffects.description: While not common for creatine, users of other long-term supplements (like NAC) warn about monitoring trace minerals like zinc.

sideEffects.label: Hair/Skin Changes:
sideEffects.description: Anecdotal reports of changes are rare but frequently discussed; scientific literature generally does not support a causal link.

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Over-the-counter and widely available globally as a dietary supplement.

availability.label: Form Preference:
availability.description: Creatine Monohydrate remains the gold standard for cost-effectiveness and proven research results.
